context.// domain config: hosted_zone_id is the Route 53 zone we delegated from Squarespace; domain_name is the FQDN the chat UI will live at. Both are needed for the Cognito + HTTPS setup. See agent README 'Setting up a domain and SSO' for how to provision the hosted zone.
context.// agent-runtime config: Values below are read by the Compute stack via self.node.try_get_context() and injected into the agent task's env. Override per-deploy with --context key=value, or pin account-specific values (role ARNs, S3 bucket URIs) in ~/.cdk.json so they don't end up in the repo.
context.// cluster + sandbox config: ASG-of-EC2 cluster shape and sandbox-pool sizing. Sandbox memory of 3072 MiB on a t3.medium (~3700 MiB usable) implicitly enforces 1 sandbox per EC2 instance — two sandboxes can't fit on the same host. The intent is one role per instance: instance 1 = agent+frontend, instance 2 = Phoenix (added M1), instance 3 = warm sandbox, instance 4 = headroom for pool refill while a session has a sandbox claimed. asg_min=4 + sandbox_pool_size=1 keeps refill working under one active session; ECS managed scaling does NOT cover this because sync RunTask RESOURCE:MEMORY failures never reach PROVISIONING and so don't trigger scale-up. Bump asg_min toward asg_max if concurrent sessions ever exceed 1.
